task.title: Harden semantic build traceability and change classification
task.summary: Require semantic changes to trace to accepted builds and add change-class routing rules for product, system, task, code bugfix, maintenance/audit, security, and publication changes.

goal.outcome: Task closure, implementation validation, and publication cannot proceed for semantic changes without accepted upstream build traceability and change-class propagation evidence.
acceptance: Build/roadmap/task APIs model change class and upstream accepted build refs where required.
Graph flags semantic code/docs/roadmap changes without accepted build coverage.
Task-close blocks or warns according to policy when accepted build traceability is missing.
Feedback loop docs/skill use Change row terminology consistently.
non_goals: Do not force a new build for generated/runtime/mechanical-only changes.
verification: codewiki_state/graph shows missing-build drift in fixture

loop_start.reason: Compiler loops start from source refs and build handoffs, not prior loop chat memory.
evidence: new session id or recorded context reset

validation.reason: Implementation validation must not reuse builder thought context and must cite checked content proof.

next_loop.reason: The next gateway must independently validate implementation evidence and cite checked content proof.

summary: Implemented TASK-082 semantic build traceability and change classification enforcement across build/task APIs, graph traceability, validation policy, docs, and smoke coverage.

evidence: tests/smoke/alignment-graph.test.mjs covers graph semantic dirty-path gaps and accepted build coverage.
tests/smoke/loop-isolation-policy.test.mjs covers build traceability fields, implementation validation blocking missing accepted planning refs, and task-close warning behavior.
tester.boundary: derive tests or test-design evidence before code changes where practical

evidence: src/application/builds.ts adds change_class/traceability metadata, accepted upstream build checks, and validation traceability policy.
src/application/graph.ts adds semantic changed-path classification and traceability gap views/reconciliation routing.
src/application/roadmap.ts, state.ts, state-builders.ts, types.ts, and schemas.ts surface change_class through roadmap/task APIs.
notes: Existing task-close validations without source build remain passable but carry a semantic traceability warning unless traceability-required policy is requested, preserving compatibility while enforcing strict implementation validation.
Non-semantic generated/runtime/mechanical change classes are modeled as bypassable for accepted upstream build requirements.
builder.boundary: change code until tests, roadmap acceptance, and required checks pass

test_design_evidence: tests/smoke/alignment-graph.test.mjs covers graph semantic dirty-path gaps and accepted build coverage.
tests/smoke/loop-isolation-policy.test.mjs covers build traceability fields, implementation validation blocking missing accepted planning refs, and task-close warning behavior.
code_change_evidence: src/application/builds.ts adds change_class/traceability metadata, accepted upstream build checks, and validation traceability policy.
src/application/graph.ts adds semantic changed-path classification and traceability gap views/reconciliation routing.
src/application/roadmap.ts, state.ts, state-builders.ts, types.ts, and schemas.ts surface change_class through roadmap/task APIs.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Build/roadmap/task APIs model change class and upstream accepted build refs where required.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: Added CHANGE_CLASS_VALUES/types/schema fields for build and task inputs; build writers emit change_class and traceability with upstream_build_refs/accepted_build_refs; roadmap create/update/read/state surfaces change_class; loop-isolation smoke asserts implementation build traceability requires accepted planning build and captures planning ref.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Graph flags semantic code/docs/roadmap changes without accepted build coverage.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: buildGraph now classifies dirty semantic paths, exposes views.traceability.semantic_change_rows/gaps, and adds reconcile:semantic-build items when no accepted build covers a changed semantic path; alignment-graph smoke asserts dirty src/application/graph.ts gap and accepted implementation build coverage.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Task-close blocks or warns according to policy when accepted build traceability is missing.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: writeValidationReport now evaluates semantic traceability for implementation/task-close/publication profiles; implementation pass blocks missing accepted planning build refs; task-close reports warn without source build and can block under traceability-required policy or when supplied source build lacks accepted upstream traceability; loop-isolation smoke covers block and warning.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Feedback loop docs/skill use Change row terminology consistently.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: Updated .codewiki/kb/system/builds.md, .codewiki/kb/system/compilers.md, and skills/codewiki/loops/feedback.md to use Change row / Change row table terminology.

closure_brief.user_intent: Proceed with TASK-082: harden semantic build traceability and change classification so semantic changes cannot validate/close/publish without accepted build lineage.
implemented_changes: Added change class vocabulary and API fields to build and roadmap/task schemas and shared types.
Emitted build traceability metadata with upstream and accepted build refs from all compiler build writers.
Hardened implementation validation so semantic implementation builds require accepted source planning build refs instead of merely any planning array.
Added semantic traceability checks for implementation, task-close, publication, publish, and release validation profiles, with task-close warning compatibility for missing source builds.
Added graph semantic dirty-path classification, traceability gap views, and reconciliation routing for semantic changes lacking accepted build coverage.
Updated feedback/build/compiler docs and skill guidance to use Change row terminology and mention accepted semantic build traceability.
acceptance_evidence: All four TASK-082 acceptance criteria mapped in acceptance_mapping.
Targeted and full smoke/feature/package checks passed.
non_goals_preserved: Generated/runtime/mechanical-only changes are modeled as non-semantic bypass cases; no build required for those classes when policy allows.
remaining_risks: Fresh validation and task-close proof still required before closing.

risks: codewiki_state currently reports one pre-existing feedback-block validation warning unrelated to TASK-082; implementation evidence and tests passed.

restore.note: Restored history is reference material until promoted into active knowledge or roadmap truth.

handoff.fallback: Use codewiki_state refresh=true and this implementation_build; do not rely on chat transcript memory.

commit.title: chore(codewiki): record TASK-082 Harden semantic build traceability and change classification implementation evidence
commit.body:
Implemented TASK-082 semantic build traceability and change classification enforcement across build/task APIs, graph traceability, validation policy, docs, and smoke coverage.

Checks: npm run typecheck, node tests/smoke/alignment-graph.test.mjs, node tests/smoke/loop-isolation-policy.test.mjs, npm run test:smoke, npm run test:features, npm run check:architecture, npm run test:pack, npm test
Validation: no durable validation refs recorded.
Remote publication requires explicit approval; this build is recommendation-only.
